steps.title: Summarize the experiment
steps.content: The teachers gave out the same number of rewards, but one counted aloud and one did not. Most children chose the counting teacher as fairer, suggesting that the method of distribution shaped their fairness judgment.

steps.title: Choose the broad main idea
steps.content: A claims children cannot understand fairness until age six, B makes the study about teaching math, and C says children expect rewards for caring about fairness. The main idea is that how rewards are handed out can affect perceived fairness. The answer is D.
